rationale: Both correctly identify the duplicate-of-#4808 disposition and the fix rationale (dead get_cache_scale call, remap already handled), matching the terse ground-truth resolution. X undermines itself by asserting '#4808 was closed without merging' with a specific claim about mergedAt being null, then admitting it couldn't actually verify main — this reads as a fabricated/unverified complication that contradicts the plain 'duplicate, closed' resolution and adds confusing, unsubstantiated follow-up asks. Y instead verifies the current state of main directly (grep for the call, current comment content, test file), correctly reports #4808 as merged, and gives a clean, actionable answer (root cause, fix, verification commands, linked issues) that closely mirrors what the thread actually needed.
